acts.summary: Establishes a film classification system for Alberta, requiring all films, videos, and video games to be classified before distribution or exhibition. Creates distribution licensing requirements and sets out enforcement powers.
acts.full_text:
Film and Video Classification Act

Chapter F-11.5 — RSA 2000

Definitions

1 In this Act,

(a) "applicant" means an applicant for a distribution licence;

(b) "associate" means a person, including an officer and a director, who performs services related to the management of the business of a distributor;

(c) "classification agency" means a person or body designated under the regulations that classifies video films;

(d) "distribution licence" means a licence issued under section 6;

(e) "distributor" means a person who distributes films to theatres;

(f) "Executive Director" means the Executive Director appointed under section 3;

(g) "film" means a cinematographic film, video film or any other medium specified in the regulations from which moving visual images may be produced;

(h) "former Act" means the Amusements Act, RSA 2000 c A-40;

(i) "inspector" means (i) the Executive Director, (ii) a member of a municipal police force, (iii) a member of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police, or (iv) a person appointed by the Minister under section 14 as an inspector;

(j) "Minister" means the Minister determined under section 16 of the Government Organization Act as the Minister responsible for this Act;

(k) "theatre" means a building or hall or any other place, including an open-air place, where films are exhibited to members of the public who have paid an admission fee;

(l) "video exchange" means a retail establishment of any kind where video films are sold, rented or otherwise made available to the public;

(m) "video film" includes a videotape, videocassette, videodisc and video game;

(n) "video game" means an object or device that stores recorded data, receives data generated by a user, and creates an interactive game capable of being played through a computer, gaming system or other technology.

Purposes of Act

2 The purposes of this Act are (a) to provide for a comprehensive procedure for the classification and regulation of films, and (b) to provide for the dissemination of information concerning the nature and content of films.

Appointment of Executive Director

3 There shall be appointed, in accordance with the Public Service Act, an Executive Director and any other employees that the Minister considers necessary to carry out the purposes of this Act.

Powers and duties of Executive Director

4(1) The Executive Director may (a) administer this Act and the regulations, (b) provide any services and undertake any projects to carry out the purposes of this Act, (c) classify films to be exhibited in a theatre, (d) exempt a film or type of film from the requirement for classification, and (e) perform any other prescribed duties.

(2) The Executive Director's power to classify films includes the power to reclassify films.

Delegation of Executive Director's powers

5 The Executive Director may in writing delegate any of the Executive Director's powers, duties or functions under this Act to any employee of the Government of Alberta.

Distribution licence required

6(1) No person shall distribute a film for the purposes of exhibition in a theatre unless the person holds a distribution licence issued by the Executive Director.

(2) An application for a distribution licence must be made to the Executive Director accompanied with a licence fee in the amount prescribed by the regulations.

(3) The Executive Director may refuse to grant a distribution licence if (a) the applicant fails to pay the licence fee, (b) the applicant includes false information, or (c) the applicant has contravened this Act.

(4) A distribution licence expires on December 31 next following the date of issue.

(5) A distribution licence is non-transferable.

Collection of personal information

7 The Executive Director may collect and disclose personal information about an applicant for the purposes of determining eligibility to hold a distribution licence.

Suspension or cancellation of licence

8 After providing the licence holder with an opportunity to be heard, the Executive Director may suspend or cancel a distribution licence.

Distribution of unclassified film prohibited

9 No distributor shall distribute a film for exhibition in a theatre unless the film has been classified by the Executive Director or exempted from classification.

Exhibition of unclassified film prohibited

10 No person shall exhibit a film in a theatre unless the film has been classified or exempted.

Admission of persons prohibited

11 No person shall admit into a theatre any person who is of a class prohibited from viewing a film of that classification.

Sale of unclassified video film prohibited

12 No video exchange operator shall sell, rent or make available a video film unless it has been classified by a classification agency or exempted.

Access by minors to adult video films prohibited

13(1) No video exchange operator shall sell, rent or make available to a minor an adult video film.

Appointment of inspectors

14 The Minister may appoint any person as an inspector for the purposes of this Act.

Authority to enter and inspect

15 For the purposes of administering and enforcing this Act, an inspector may at any reasonable time and without a warrant enter and inspect any theatre, video exchange or other premises where a film is distributed, exhibited, sold, rented or made available to the public.

Offences

16 A person who gives false information in a licence application, obstructs an inspector, or contravenes this Act is guilty of an offence.

Penalties

17(1) A person guilty of an offence is liable (a) in the case of an individual, to a fine of not more than $10,000 or imprisonment for not more than 2 years less a day, or both, and (b) in the case of a corporation, to a fine of not more than $100,000.

Classification reports

18 The Executive Director shall publish a classification report for each film classified.

Regulations

19(1) The Lieutenant Governor in Council may make regulations respecting licensing of distributors, classification schemes, submission of films, fees, designation of classification agencies, appeals, and any other matter necessary for carrying out the purposes of this Act.

sections.title: Appointment of Director
sections.description: The Minister appoints a Director of Film Classification responsible for administering the classification system, including reviewing and rating films, videos, and video games for public distribution.

services.name: Submit a film or video for classification

sections.title: Classification of films
sections.description: Every film must be classified before it is distributed, exhibited, or offered for sale in Alberta. The Director assigns classifications based on content, including age restrictions and content advisories.

services.name: Submit a film or video for classification

sections.title: Distribution licences
sections.description: No person shall distribute a film in Alberta without a valid distribution licence. Licences are issued by the Director and must be renewed annually.

services.name: Apply for a film distribution licence

sections.title: Video game classification
sections.description: Video games distributed in Alberta must bear an approved classification rating. The Director may accept classifications from recognized industry rating organizations.

services.name: Submit a film or video for classification

sections.title: Enforcement and penalties
sections.description: Provisions for enforcement of the Act, including powers of inspection, seizure, and penalties for distributing unclassified films or operating without a licence.

services.name: Submit a film or video for classification
Apply for a film distribution licence

acts.summary: Provides for the identification, preservation, and protection of Alberta's historical, archaeological, palaeontological, and natural heritage resources. Establishes designation and permitting systems and governs provincial museums, archives, and historic sites.
acts.full_text:
Historical Resources Act

Chapter H-9 — RSA 2000

Part 1: Historical Resources Generally

Definitions

1 In this Act,

(a) "archaeological object" means any object that is primarily of value for its prehistoric, historic, cultural, scientific, aesthetic or educational significance and is or was buried or partially buried in land or is deposited on or below the surface of any watercourse or permanent body of water;

(b) "archaeological site" means a site where an archaeological object is found;

(c) "historic resource" means any work of nature or of humans that is primarily of value for its palaeontological, archaeological, prehistoric, historic, cultural, natural, scientific or aesthetic interest including, but not limited to, a palaeontological, archaeological, prehistoric, historic or natural site, structure or object;

(d) "Minister" means the Minister determined under section 16 of the Government Organization Act as the Minister responsible for this Act;

(e) "Municipal Historic Resource" means a structure designated by a municipality under section 26;

(f) "palaeontological object" means any object that is primarily of value for its palaeontological interest;

(g) "Provincial Historic Resource" means a historic resource designated by the Minister under section 20;

(h) "Registered Historic Resource" means a historic resource entered on the register under section 19.

Duties of the Minister

2 The Minister may (a) establish, acquire, maintain, administer and operate historic sites, museums and archives; (b) acquire and preserve historic resources; (c) carry out research relating to historical resources; (d) establish programs to encourage the preservation of historic resources; (e) enter into agreements for the purposes of this Act.

Staff

3 The Minister may appoint employees as necessary for the administration of this Act.

Advisory board

4(1) The Minister may establish an advisory board to advise the Minister on matters relating to historic resources.

(2) The advisory board consists of members appointed by the Minister.

Grants and financial assistance

5 The Minister may make grants and provide financial assistance for the preservation and interpretation of historic resources.

Property of the Crown

6 All archaeological objects and palaeontological objects found in Alberta on or after April 1, 1978 are the property of the Crown in right of Alberta.

Part 3: Historic Resource Management

Notice of development

16 Where a person proposes any development on Crown land or on any land to which a surface disposition applies, and the development may affect a historic resource, the person shall notify the Minister of the proposed development.

Historic Resources Impact Assessment

17 The Minister may require any person to prepare a Historic Resources Impact Assessment of the effect of proposed development on historic resources.

Register of Historic Resources

19 The Minister shall establish and maintain a register of historic resources that the Minister considers to be of significance to Alberta.

Designation of Provincial Historic Resources

20(1) The Minister may, by order, designate any historic resource as a Provincial Historic Resource.

(2) Before making a designation, the Minister shall give the owner notice and an opportunity to be heard.

(3) A Provincial Historic Resource shall not be destroyed, disturbed, altered, or otherwise dealt with without the written consent of the Minister.

Effect of Provincial designation

21 On designation of a Provincial Historic Resource, the Minister shall cause a notice of the designation to be registered in the relevant land titles office.

Maintenance of Provincial Historic Resources

22 The owner of a Provincial Historic Resource shall maintain the resource in a manner satisfactory to the Minister.

Municipal Historic Resources

26(1) A municipality may, by bylaw, designate any historic resource within the municipality as a Municipal Historic Resource.

(2) Before making a designation, the municipality shall give the owner notice and an opportunity to be heard.

(3) A Municipal Historic Resource shall not be destroyed, disturbed, altered, or otherwise dealt with without the written consent of the municipality.

Excavation permits

31(1) No person shall excavate or carry out research on any archaeological site or palaeontological site without a permit issued by the Minister.

(2) The Minister may attach conditions to a permit.

(3) The Minister may suspend or cancel a permit if the holder contravenes a condition of the permit or this Act.

Provincial Archives

35(1) The Minister shall establish, maintain and administer the Provincial Archives of Alberta.

(2) The Provincial Archives shall collect, preserve and make available records of significance to Alberta.

Heritage impact assessments

37 The Minister may require a heritage impact assessment before approving any development that may affect a historic resource, ensuring impacts are identified and mitigated.

Part 5: General

Temporary stop orders

49 The Minister may issue a temporary stop order prohibiting any activity that may damage or destroy a historic resource.

Offences and penalties

51(1) A person who contravenes this Act is guilty of an offence and liable (a) in the case of an individual, to a fine of not more than $50,000 or imprisonment for not more than one year, or both; (b) in the case of a corporation, to a fine of not more than $1,000,000.

Crown bound

53 This Act binds the Crown.

sections.title: Definitions and scope
sections.description: Defines historical resources broadly to include archaeological, palaeontological, historic, natural, and cultural objects and sites. Establishes the scope of protection under the Act.

services.name: Manage heritage policy and provincial collections

sections.description: Establishes an advisory board to advise the Minister on matters related to historical resources, including designation of resources and policy development.

sections.description: The Minister may designate any historic resource as a Provincial Historic Resource. Designation provides legal protection against alteration or destruction without ministerial consent.

sections.description: A municipality may designate a historic resource within its boundaries as a Municipal Historic Resource, providing local protection and potential access to conservation funding.

sections.description: No person shall conduct archaeological or palaeontological research or excavation without a permit issued by the Minister. Permits set conditions to ensure scientific standards and resource protection.

acts.full_name: Alberta Foundation for the Arts Act
acts.summary: Establishes the Alberta Foundation for the Arts as a provincial corporation to support and promote the arts in Alberta through grants, awards, and other programs for artists and arts organizations.
acts.full_text:
Alberta Foundation for the Arts Act

Chapter A-19 — RSA 2000

Definitions

1 In this Act,

(a) "arts" means (i) the literary, performing, visual and media arts, and (ii) anything prescribed by the regulations;

(b) "Foundation" means the Alberta Foundation for the Arts established by this Act;

(c) "Minister" means the Minister determined under section 16 of the Government Organization Act as the Minister responsible for this Act.

Foundation

2 The Alberta Foundation for the Arts is established as a corporation.

Purposes

3 The purposes of the Foundation are (a) to support and contribute to the development of and to promote the arts in Alberta; (b) to provide persons and organizations with the opportunity to participate in the arts; (c) to foster and promote the enjoyment of works of art by Alberta artists; (d) to collect, preserve and display works of art by Alberta artists; (e) to encourage artists resident in Alberta in their work.

Crown agent

4 The Foundation is an agent of the Crown in right of Alberta.

Powers

5 The Foundation may, subject to the regulations, (a) acquire real or personal property, including works of art; (b) act as trustee of property acquired in trust; (c) hold, preserve, maintain, renovate, restore and manage its property; (d) dispose of property by sale, lease or other manner; (e) make grants to any person or organization; (f) publish and distribute materials about the Foundation; (g) hire employees, consultants and advisors; (h) conduct fund-raising campaigns; (i) make and maintain banking arrangements; (j) draw, make, accept, endorse, execute and issue negotiable instruments; (k) exercise any power given by the regulations.

Directions by Minister

6 The Minister may give directions to the Foundation for the purposes of (a) providing priorities and guidelines for it to follow, and (b) co-ordinating the work of the Foundation with the programs, policies and work of the Government and public and private organizations.

Composition of Foundation

7(1) The Foundation consists of members appointed by the Lieutenant Governor in Council.

(2) The Lieutenant Governor in Council shall designate from among the members a chair and one or more vice-chairs.

(3) The Minister may designate an employee of the Government as secretary of the Foundation.

Remuneration and expenses

8(1) The Foundation shall pay its members remuneration and travelling, living and other expenses incurred in the course of their duties.

(2) Remuneration and expenses must be determined in accordance with applicable regulations under the Alberta Public Agencies Governance Act, or by the Minister if no regulations are applicable.

Bylaws

9(1) The Foundation may make bylaws (a) governing the administration of the Foundation; (b) governing the making of grants; (c) relating to any matter necessary for carrying out its purposes.

(2) A bylaw does not become effective until approved by the Minister.

Government employees

10 If the Minister considers it necessary, the Minister shall provide to the Foundation the services of employees of the Government to carry out its work.

Funds

11(1) Money received by the Foundation from any source constitutes the funds of the Foundation.

(2) The income of the funds accrues to and forms part of those funds.

(3) Expenditures and grants must be paid from the funds of the Foundation.

Management and pooled investment of funds

12 The Foundation may be a participant under section 40 of the Financial Administration Act.

Fiscal year

13 The fiscal year of the Foundation is April 1 to the following March 31.

Annual report

14(1) The Foundation shall, as soon as possible after the end of the fiscal year, provide the Minister with a report summarizing its operations and including audited financial statements.

(2) The Minister shall lay a copy of the report before the Legislative Assembly.

Regulations

15 The Minister may make regulations (a) prescribing anything to be part of the arts; (b) restricting and regulating the powers and duties of the Foundation; (c) establishing additional powers and duties of the Foundation.

sections.title: Establishment of the Foundation
sections.description: Establishes the Alberta Foundation for the Arts as a corporation with the mandate to support the arts in Alberta and promote public interest in and awareness of the arts.

services.name: Develop arts and cultural industries strategy

sections.title: Objects and powers
sections.description: The Foundation may provide grants and awards to artists and arts organizations, acquire and manage an art collection, conduct research, and do anything necessary to promote and support the arts.

acts.summary: Recognizes the professional status of artists in Alberta and establishes principles for the fair treatment of arts professionals. Affirms that artists contribute to the social, cultural, and economic well-being of the province.
acts.full_text:
Arts Professions Recognition Act

Chapter A-44.2 — SA 2021

Preamble: WHEREAS Alberta artists are valuable professional members of society; WHEREAS the Government of Alberta is committed to promoting the creation and production of artistic work and ensuring fair compensation to artists; NOW THEREFORE Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Legislative Assembly of Alberta, enacts as follows:

Definitions

1 In this Act,

(a) "artist" means a person who practices art in one or more of the following fields: (i) literary arts, including writing, translating, illustrating and editing; (ii) visual arts, including painting, sculpting, printmaking, drawing, textile arts and photography; (iii) electronic and media arts, including filmmaking, audio and video recording, and multimedia; (iv) performing arts, including singing, acting, dancing, directing, choreographing and conducting;

(b) "professional artist" means an artist who meets one or more of the following criteria: (i) is recognized as a professional by other artists working in the same artistic field; (ii) is in the process of becoming an artist according to the practice of the artistic field; (iii) has a body of work that has been presented in a public context; (iv) has received professional training in the artistic field; (v) is a member of a professional artists' association; (vi) has received copyright or royalties for artistic work;

(c) "artists' association" means a group of professional artists that has as one of its objectives the promotion of the economic and professional interests of its members;

(d) "public entity" means (i) the Crown in right of Alberta, (ii) a Crown agency, or (iii) any other entity prescribed by the regulations.

Undertakings by public entities

2 In matters relating to artistic work, every public entity shall respect the scale agreements, working conditions and protocols established by artists' associations.

Written contracts

3(1) A public entity that contracts with a professional artist for artistic work, or for the presentation, circulation or dissemination of the artist's artistic works, shall use a written contract.

(2) A written contract referred to in subsection (1) shall comply with any requirements prescribed by the regulations.

Regulations

4 The Lieutenant Governor in Council may make regulations (a) prescribing additional fields of artistic endeavour; (b) prescribing entities as public entities; (c) prescribing requirements for written contracts; (d) exempting contracts from the requirement for a written contract; (e) respecting any other matter necessary for carrying out the purposes of this Act.

sections.title: Recognition of arts professions
sections.description: Recognizes that artists practising in Alberta are professionals who contribute to the social, cultural, and economic well-being of the province.

services.name: Learn about your rights as a professional artist

sections.title: Principles
sections.description: Establishes principles for the fair treatment of artists, including the right to freedom of expression, fair compensation, and respect for the contribution of artists to Alberta's culture.

services.name: Learn about your rights as a professional artist
